macro_summary: Markets enter a historically dense 48-hour event window with FOMC rate decision on July 29 (25-30% hike probability) coinciding with mega-cap earnings from MSFT, AAPL, and others. AI infrastructure spending remains the dominant macro theme, with global chip sales +104% YoY (May SIA data) and hyperscalers collectively guiding $600B+ in 2026 AI capex. The SOX index is rebounding after a ~9% pullback last week, with Asian chipmakers up 3%+ overnight on AI buildout flows. Rate uncertainty keeps the risk/reward balance tilted toward defined-risk options structures over outright stock exposure.
screening_note: 6 independent overnight screening runs analyzed. Minimum threshold to qualify: 2 appearances. 5 tickers cleared the bar. MSFT and SKHY achieved perfect 6/6 consensus; AAPL and NVDA appeared in 5/6 runs; QQQ in 2/6.
key_events: FOMC rate decision July 29, 2026 — 25-30% probability of hike
MSFT Q4 FY2026 earnings July 29 after close — consensus $87.7B revenue / $4.24 EPS
SKHY first Nasdaq ADR earnings print July 29; Cheongju AI memory packaging plant expansion announcement today
AAPL Q3 FY2026 earnings July 30 after close
Intel earnings July 23 after close — near-term NVDA read-through

ideas.name: Microsoft Corporation
ideas.thesis: MSFT appeared in all 6 overnight screens with unanimous high conviction — the strongest signal of the session. The stock is down ~20% YTD and trading at ~21-22x forward earnings, its cheapest valuation since 2023, despite 97 analysts carrying an average price target of $589-592 (~56% implied upside). July 29 earnings are the hard catalyst, with Azure AI growth and Copilot monetization as the primary beat/raise drivers; consensus sets a low bar and any upside guide could trigger meaningful multiple re-rating.
ideas.catalyst: Q4 FY2026 earnings report July 29, 2026 after market close; consensus $87.7B revenue / $4.24 EPS
ideas.edge: Forward P/E of ~21-22x NTM is cheapest since 2023 vs. historical ~30x; 97-analyst average price target of $589-592 implies ~56% upside from current depressed level
ideas.bull_case: Azure AI acceleration and Copilot monetization beat drives earnings upside and forces multiple re-rating toward historical 30x, closing a significant portion of the 56% target gap.
ideas.bear_case: Elevated AI capex concerns weigh on margin outlook, guide disappoints, and the stock remains stuck in its compressed multiple range despite a beat on headline numbers.

ideas.name: SK Hynix Inc. (Nasdaq ADR)
ideas.thesis: SKHY appeared in all 6 overnight screens, making it a co-top consensus idea alongside MSFT. The ADR listed on Nasdaq just ~2 weeks ago at $149 and last closed at ~$165, still well into post-IPO price-discovery with heavy institutional interest. The 12-month analyst consensus target of $281.67 implies ~70% upside, anchored by SK Hynix's dominant HBM/AI memory positioning and management guidance that global memory shortages will persist beyond 2030. July 29 marks the first-ever Nasdaq earnings print, a meaningful de-risking milestone. The company is also announcing expanded Cheongju AI memory packaging plant investment today.
ideas.catalyst: First Nasdaq ADR earnings print July 29, 2026; Cheongju AI memory packaging plant expansion announcement July 23
ideas.edge: 12-month analyst average target $281.67 vs. ~$165 last close = ~70% implied upside; IPO priced at $149 just 2 weeks ago, still in price-discovery with AI HBM demand as structural tailwind; Asian AI memory peers up 3%+ overnight
ideas.bull_case: Strong inaugural earnings print validates the HBM/AI memory thesis, institutional demand accelerates post-lockup, and the stock re-rates meaningfully toward the $281 analyst consensus as the memory shortage narrative gains further traction.
ideas.bear_case: Post-IPO volatility is extreme and liquidity is nascent; a disappointing first print or broader risk-off from FOMC/rate shock could send the stock sharply back toward the $149 IPO price with no options floor yet firmly established.

ideas.name: NVIDIA Corporation
ideas.thesis: NVDA appeared in 5 of 6 overnight screens and is the highest-conviction AI infrastructure beneficiary in this earnings cycle. Trading at ~$202.78 against a 68-analyst consensus target of $302.73 (~49% implied upside), the stock benefits from a stacked read-through calendar this week — Intel tonight, MSFT Azure guide July 29, Amazon July 30 — before its own August 26 print. Blackwell GPU supply is up ~40% in Q2 per KeyBanc, and hyperscalers collectively guiding $600B+ in 2026 AI capex directly supports NVDA's revenue trajectory.
ideas.catalyst: Q2 FY2027 earnings August 26, 2026; near-term hyperscaler capex read-throughs from Intel (July 23 AMC), MSFT (July 29), and AAPL/AMZN (July 30)
ideas.edge: 68-analyst consensus target of $302.73 vs. ~$202.78 current price = ~49% implied upside; 85% of analysts rate Buy; Blackwell GPU supply up ~40% Q2 per KeyBanc; global chip sales +104% YoY (May 2026 SIA)
ideas.bull_case: MSFT and Amazon deliver strong AI capex guidance July 29-30, acting as a sentiment catalyst well ahead of the Aug 26 print; Blackwell ramp accelerates and NVDA re-rates back toward $300+.
ideas.bear_case: Hyperscaler capex commentary disappoints or signals digestion pause, dampening sentiment into the Aug 26 print; elevated IV around earnings and geopolitical export control risks remain overhangs.

ideas.name: Apple Inc.
ideas.thesis: AAPL appeared in 5 of 6 overnight screens with mixed high/medium conviction. The stock has pulled back ~9% over the past month to ~$284 ahead of a hard July 30 earnings catalyst, creating a potentially attractive pre-earnings entry. Citi and HSBC both raised targets in July to $365-366, implying ~28% upside from current levels, citing AI opportunity and market-share gains. The fall 2026 Mac AI overhaul (Bloomberg-reported) serves as an additional near-term product cycle catalyst. Defined-risk call spreads are the preferred expression given the rich forward P/E of ~39.5x and elevated IV into the print.
ideas.catalyst: Q3 FY2026 earnings July 30, 2026 after close; Bloomberg-reported fall 2026 Mac AI overhaul as near-term product catalyst
ideas.edge: Stock at ~$284, down ~9% in past month; Citi ($365) and HSBC ($366) both raised targets in July — consensus ~$315-366 range implying 11-29% upside; Services segment at $31B/qtr with 32% margins provides earnings floor
ideas.bull_case: Services growth sustains margins, AI Mac refresh cycle drives hardware upside, and fresh analyst upgrades to $365-366 attract re-rating momentum after a clean July 30 beat-and-raise.
ideas.bear_case: China demand weakness or tariff headwinds disappoint on iPhone units; at ~39.5x forward P/E a miss on either revenue or guidance sends the stock materially lower despite the pullback.

ideas.name: Invesco QQQ Trust (Nasdaq-100 ETF)
ideas.thesis: QQQ appeared in 2 of 6 runs — the minimum threshold — flagged primarily as a defined-risk hedge or income vehicle rather than a directional idea. The Nasdaq-100 faces its highest binary event density of the Q2 season in a 48-hour window: FOMC July 29 (25-30% hike probability) colliding with the MSFT, META, and AAPL earnings cluster July 29-30. Elevated IV into this window creates opportunity for an iron condor or put spread to capture premium if markets digest the events without a macro shock; alternatively, a call spread benefits from the Goldman constructive H2 base case.
ideas.catalyst: FOMC rate decision July 29, 2026; MSFT/META earnings July 29, AAPL July 30 — highest event density of the Q2 earnings season
ideas.edge: Dual binary events (Fed + mega-cap earnings cluster) compress into 48 hours; elevated IV typical of this window makes options premium capture via iron condor or defined-risk spread attractive; macro hold scenario at 3.5-3.75% rates historically supportive of tech multiple expansion
ideas.bull_case: FOMC holds rates, mega-cap earnings beat on AI capex and cloud growth, and the Nasdaq-100 breaks higher with tech multiples expanding into a rate-stable H2 environment.
ideas.bear_case: Surprise rate hike or earnings guidance disappointment from MSFT/AAPL triggers a vol spike and Nasdaq selloff, punishing long exposure; the compressed event window amplifies downside velocity.
